title: HBR-VIS-005 verification-gap blocking-receipt path
goal: Per HBR-VIS-005, when a validator discovers a UI/automation surface that cannot be captured or driven by headless automation, it must record a blocking verification gap in WP OPEN_BLOCKERS with the exact missing surface; do not silently certify the work. Open a follow-up WP for the missing automation hook.
expected_diff_shape: New module/script .GOV/roles_shared/scripts/hbr-vis-gap-emit.mjs producing a HBR_VIS_GAP receipt + writing the OPEN_BLOCKERS entry; Rust helper in handshake_core for runtime emission; tests covering both build-time and runtime emission paths.
unblock_conditions: MT-006 receipt schema + MT-022 console scan + MT-023 capture matrix exist so the gap emitter has the right context to attach to.
implementation_notes: Per HBR-VIS-005 evidence_kind=open_blocker_with_missing_surface_named. When MT-022 scan or MT-023 capture-matrix encounters a UI element with no automation handle (e.g., a canvas-rendered control that has no CDP-queryable accessibility tree node, or a native-Win32 child window injected via webview-rust-side that CDP cannot drive), it must: (1) emit a typed `HBR_VIS_GAP` receipt with `{ receipt_kind: "HBR_VIS_GAP", schema_version: 1, receipt_uuid: Uuid v7, wp_id, surface_name, surface_path, gap_class: "no_cdp_handle"|"native_child_window"|"opaque_canvas"|"shadow_root_inaccessible"|"other", proposed_followup_wp: Option<String>, evidence_pointer: Option<String> }`, (2) append a blocker entry to the WP's OPEN_BLOCKERS list (the WP packet.json gets an `open_blockers` array; this MT defines the schema if not already present). The script CLI: `node .GOV/roles_shared/scripts/hbr-vis-gap-emit.mjs --wp <id> --surface <name> --gap-class <class>` produces the receipt + writes the OPEN_BLOCKERS entry. Rust runtime: `VisGap::emit(packet: &mut Packet, gap: VisGap)`. Tests: emit a gap, assert (a) receipt produced in canonical JSON validates against schema, (b) packet's open_blockers array contains the new entry, (c) gov-check umbrella detects open_blockers and blocks PASS closure (this validates the integration with MT-003 matrix check — MT-003 must also check open_blockers count as a separate fail condition).
risk_if_missed: Without HBR-VIS-005 path, validators silently certify visual coverage despite gaps; the WP ships with unverifiable surfaces.
validator_focus: Gap receipt validates against schema; OPEN_BLOCKERS entry is structured (not freeform prose); gov-check umbrella blocks closure when open_blockers count > 0.

minimum_controls: open_blockers entries are structured (schema-validated), not freeform prose.
gov-check umbrella checks open_blockers count and blocks PASS when > 0.
Follow-up WP id is optional but encouraged (the receipt schema supports it).
